title: Hyderabadi Biryani
description: Fragrant rice layered with marinated meat and aromatic spices. A celebratory one-pot meal.

tips: Longer marination (overnight) significantly improves flavor. Plan ahead!
subSteps: Cut chicken into uniform chunks (1.5 to 2 inches).
Mix yogurt with ginger-garlic paste and salt.
Add green chillies and half the cilantro-mint mixture.
Add chicken and marinate for at least 2 hours (overnight is better).
Keep refrigerated.
Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes in water.
Slice onions into thin rings.
Deep fry onions in ghee until golden brown and crispy (reserve for garnish).
Keep cilantro and mint leaves ready for garnishing.
Slit green chillies lengthwise.
Measure out bay leaves, cinnamon stick, green cardamom pods, black cardamom pods, cloves, and cumin seeds.
Arrange all spices in small bowls for easy access during cooking.

steps.instruction: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices.
subSteps: Mix yogurt with ginger-garlic paste and salt.
Add green chillies and half the cilantro-mint mixture.
Add chicken chunks and mix well.
Let marinate for at least 2 hours.
Longer marination (4-6 hours or overnight) gives better flavor.

tips: Rice should still have a bite to it — it will fully cook in the biryani.
subSteps: Bring water to boil with whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves).
Add soaked rice and salt.
Cook until 70% done (rice should be firm, not fully cooked).
Drain immediately.

steps.instruction: Layer rice and marinated meat and cook together.
tips: Dum cooking (steaming in a sealed pot) is key to biryani flavor and texture. Don't open the lid while cooking!
subSteps: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot (preferably 5-liter capacity).
Layer half the marinated chicken at the bottom.
Spread half the parboiled rice over it.
Sprinkle remaining cilantro-mint, some fried onions.
Layer remaining chicken, then remaining rice.
Top with fried onions, cilantro, and mint.
Drizzle remaining ghee on top.
Cover with aluminum foil, then place a tight-fitting lid.
Cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms.
Reduce to low heat and cook for 30 minutes (dum cooking).
Turn off heat and let it rest for 5 minutes without opening.

seo.metaTitle: Hyderabadi Biryani Recipe — Authentic One-Pot Meal
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make restaurant-quality Hyderabadi biryani at home with this detailed recipe guide.
